  "decision": "fail",

  "sound_source": "water hammer",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 1.5,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Severe water hammer (hydraulic shock) occurs immediately after the fixture valve closes.",
    "Lack of adequate pipe support, securing, or water hammer arrestors."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Install a water hammer arrestor close to the fast-closing valve.",
    "Secure loose PEX and copper piping with approved hangers and straps to prevent movement."
  ],
  "rationale": "The audio clearly demonstrates a loud, metallic banging sound (water hammer) immediately following the rapid closure of a fixture valve. This hydraulic shock can damage joints, fittings, and appliances over time, violating standard plumbing codes (IPC 604.9) which require water hammer arrestors where quick-acting valves are installed.",
  "references": [
    "IPC Section 604.9 (Water hammer)",
    "UPC Section 609.10 (Water Hammer Arrestors)"
  ]
}
